<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> In a nutshell, what I think is going on in this gospel reading is the difference between mystery and magic.<|10.40|>
<|11.84|> Our Savior, even though it sometimes appears to be the case, our Savior never promises magic.<|20.18|>
<|20.74|> Our Savior never promises magic.<|21.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But Christ does promise mystery.<|5.02|>
<|6.22|> It's the mystery of an awareness of a God who is always with us<|12.26|>
<|12.26|> when the healing comes, when the healing doesn't come.<|16.32|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> a God who is always with us.<|2.82|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> through all our joys and sorrows<|3.18|>
